title: Triplet Loss
description:
Implement Triplet Loss for metric learning.

Given an anchor, a positive (same class), and a negative (different class):

`L = mean(max(0, D(a,p) - D(a,n) + margin))`

where `D(x,y) = ||x - y||_2`.

The loss encourages `D(a,p) + margin < D(a,n)`: the anchor should be closer to the positive than the negative by at least `margin`.
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def triplet_loss(anchor: np.ndarray, positive: np.ndarray, negative: np.ndarray, margin: float = 0.2) -> float:
    """Compute triplet loss.

    Args:
        anchor: Anchor embeddings, shape (B, D)
        positive: Positive embeddings (same class as anchor), shape (B, D)
        negative: Negative embeddings (different class), shape (B, D)
        margin: Margin between positive and negative distances

    Returns:
        Mean triplet loss
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def triplet_loss(anchor: np.ndarray, positive: np.ndarray, negative: np.ndarray, margin: float = 0.2) -> float:
    anchor = np.asarray(anchor, dtype=float)
    positive = np.asarray(positive, dtype=float)
    negative = np.asarray(negative, dtype=float)
    d_ap = np.linalg.norm(anchor - positive, axis=1)
    d_an = np.linalg.norm(anchor - negative, axis=1)
    loss = np.maximum(0.0, d_ap - d_an + margin)
    return float(np.mean(loss))
explanation: Triplet loss trains embeddings so that an anchor is closer to its positive (same class) than to any negative (different class) by a margin. The margin prevents the model from collapsing all embeddings to a point. Easy triplets (D(a,n) >> D(a,p)) contribute zero loss, so hard negative mining is often needed.

test_cases.name: Easy triplet loss=0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([[0.0,0]])
p = np.array([[0.1,0]])
n = np.array([[2.0,0]])
assert triplet_loss(a, p, n, margin=0.2) == 0.0

test_cases.name: Hard triplet positive loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([[0.0,0]])
p = np.array([[1.0,0]])
n = np.array([[0.5,0]])
loss = triplet_loss(a, p, n, margin=0.2)
assert loss > 0

test_cases.name: Identical anchor-positive loss=margin if D(a,n)=0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([[0.0,0]])
p = np.array([[0.0,0]])
n = np.array([[0.0,0]])
loss = triplet_loss(a, p, n, margin=0.5)
assert abs(loss - 0.5) < 1e-9

test_cases.name: Batch mean
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
a = np.zeros((4,2))
p = np.zeros((4,2))
n = np.ones((4,2)) * 5
loss = triplet_loss(a, p, n, margin=0.2)
assert loss == 0.0

hints: Loss per triplet = max(0, D_ap - D_an + margin)
